mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and signals easier financial conditions, which lifts S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days when the cut is part of an easing cycle.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive on average after FEDFUNDS declines over any 12-month rolling window, or if the sign flips positive during sustained hiking cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above the recent trend while FEDFUNDS remains far below CPI inflation, real policy stays negative and nominal equity multiples compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above FEDFUNDS by more than 2.0pp
regime_hint: inflation overshoot with delayed policy normalization
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if returns are indistinguishable from zero after inflation-policy spread crossings.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE indicates deteriorating labor income and recession risk, which weakens expected earnings and pressures S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise episodes are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the rolling predictive correlation remains near zero for 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS signals stronger payroll income and retail demand, which should raise near-term earnings expectations and support S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint increase in PAYEMS and RSAFS does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal is negative across a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO captures rising real output and utilization, which implies stronger cyclical earnings momentum and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change turns positive after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if positive-turning INDPRO signals fail to produce higher-than-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign remains negative during repeated production recoveries.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST signals housing demand deterioration and tighter wealth effects, which should precede weaker S&P 500 performance over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% year over year
failure_condition: Reject if housing contraction episodes are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if the signal is absorbed entirely by other macro series.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of declining PAYEMS and rising UNRATE identifies a labor-market break that reduces future consumption and earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-down and UNRATE-up pattern does not predict negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of occurrences, or if either series alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a higher FEDFUNDS rate coinciding with elevated CPIAUCSL implies policy lag and real-rate pressure on valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: Fed tightening cycle with inflation persistence
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not negative on average during tightening-plus-high-inflation periods, or if the effect disappears once CPIAUCSL is controlled for.
